prevalence.source: Academia Brasileira de Neurologia / IHME GBD estimates
prevalence.trend: increasing due to population aging and improved survival

burden.mortalityRate: Increasing with age; indirect mortality common

vulnerablePopulations: patients with limited access to neurology care
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis (bradykinesia plus rest tremor/rigidity) per MDS criteria; response to levodopa supportive; imaging to rule out mimics when needed

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; diagnosis often delayed until functional impact
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Symptom control moderate with dopaminergic therapy; advanced therapy access limited outside major centers

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Neurology and movement disorder specialist access concentrated in capitals; primary care recognition variable
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Levodopa/carbidopa and dopamine agonists where appropriate; physiotherapy and speech therapy; deep brain stimulation (DBS) in reference centers
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to multidisciplinary rehab and advanced therapies (DBS, infusion therapies) within SUS outside large cities
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Core dopaminergic medications available and specialized centers exist in major academic hospitals
